title: elaphoglossum subcochleare
intro: Elaphoglossum subcochleare is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae, native to Central and South America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving skin health, and supporting immune function. Elaphoglossum subcochleare can be prepared as a tea or infusion, and in some cases, it is also used in topical applications, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
